note: Select one introductory course: Introduction to Africana Studies or Introduction to Africa. African Language courses do not count toward the minor.

Both PSCI 0200 Introduction to American Politics and BEPP 2010 American Public Policy through an Economic Lens are required.
Select 2 PSCI-side courses with the APAP attribute.
Select 2 BEPP-side courses with the APAP attribute.

minorName: American Sign Language and Deaf Studies, Minor
note: ASLD 1032 Deaf Culture is required.

minorName: American Sign Language and Deaf Studies, Minor
note: Select one: ASLD 1030 Advanced ASL and Deaf History OR ASLD 1031 Deaf Literature, Performance, Art, and Film.

minorName: American Sign Language and Deaf Studies, Minor
note: Both LING 0001 Introduction to Linguistics and LING 0600 Introduction to Sociolinguistics are required.

minorName: American Sign Language and Deaf Studies, Minor
note: ASLD 1033 ASL/Deaf Studies ABCS is required.

minorName: American Sign Language and Deaf Studies, Minor
note: Select 1 cross-disciplinary elective in consultation with the minor advisor. See http://www.ling.upenn.edu/undergraduate/minor-asl for full current approved list.
Select one introductory survey course.
Select 5 electives from ANCH 0000-4999, CLST 0000-4999, GREK 0000-4999, LATN 0000-4999, courses with AANP/ACLB attributes, or by approval of Undergrad Chair.

Select 6 ANTH-numbered courses only. No more than 4 from any one subfield: Cultural/Linguistic, Archaeology, or Human Biology. Courses with ANTH attribute but no ANTH number do NOT count for the minor.

Select 4 CAAM courses from introductory, intermediate, and advanced levels. See https://www.penn.museum/sites/caam/courses/minor-in-archaeological-science/ for full list. Contact mboileau@upenn.edu to declare.
Select 1 advanced CAAM capstone course with archaeological science research component. Contact Dr. Marie-Claude Boileau (mboileau@upenn.edu) for current approved options.
Select 1 course in the archaeology or culture of a relevant area.
Select 2 of 3 survey courses: World Art Before 1400, Renaissance to the Present, or Architect and History.
Select 4 ARTH courses at the 2000-level or above. One may be from a related field outside Art History with approval of the Undergraduate Chair.
Select either ARCH 1010 Introduction to Design or ARCH 1020 Introduction to Architecture.
Both ARCH 2010 Design Fundamentals I and ARCH 2020 Design Fundamentals II are required.
ARTH 2810 Modern Architecture, from the Enlightenment to the Present is required.
Select 1 course with the AARH (Art History) attribute. See catalog.upenn.edu/attributes/aarh/ for full list.
ASAM 0100 Introduction to Asian American Studies and ASAM 0101 Asian American Communities are both required.
Select one: ASAM 0103 Asian American Literature OR ASAM 0102 Introduction to Asian American History.
Select 2 additional ASAM courses.
Select 1 ASAM course or related course from another department, chosen in consultation with the Minor advisor.
Select 1 course with the ABEH (BIOE Historical Courses) attribute.
Select 1 course with the ABEN (BIOE Normative Courses) attribute. Students declaring Fall 2024 or later must take BIOE 4010 or BIOE 4020.
Select 1 course with the ABEP (BIOE Policy and Culture) attribute.
Select 1 course with the ABEA (BIOE Allocation) attribute.
Select 1 course with the ABEC (BIOE Clinical Ethics) attribute.
Select 1 course with the ABER (BIOE Research Ethics) attribute. No more than 3 courses total in the minor may come from any one department.

Track 1: BIOL 1121 Introduction to Biology (Molecular Biology of Life), BIOL 1123 Introductory Molecular Biology Lab, and BIOL 1124 Introductory Organismal Biology Lab. Choose Track 1 or Track 2, not both.
Track 2: BIOL 1101 Introduction to Biology A and BIOL 1102 Introduction to Biology B. Choose Track 1 or Track 2, not both.
Select 2 course units of Introductory Chemistry (ABIC attribute). Typically CHEM 1011 or 1012 + CHEM 1021 or 1022, plus lab CHEM 1101+1102.
Select 1 course from Group 1: Biochemistry, Principles of Biological Chemistry, or Cell Biology.
Required: BIOL 2210 Molecular Biology and Genetics.
Select 1 from Group 3: Human Physiology, Animal Behavior, or Molecular/Cellular Neurobiology.
Select 1 from Group 4: Evolutionary Biology or Ecology.
Track 1 students only: select 1 additional BIOL course at any level.
BIOL 2010 Cell Biology is required.
Select one: PHYS 2280 or BCHE 2280 Physical Models of Biological Systems.
CHEM 1011 or 1151 (Gen Chem I), CHEM 1021 or 1161 (Gen Chem II), and CHEM 1101+1102 General Chemistry Labs are all required (counted as ~3 CU total).
MATH 1400 Calculus I, one of MATH 1410/1510/1610 (Calculus II), and MATH 2400 or MATH 2600 (Calculus III) are all required.
Option 1: PHYS 0150 or 0170 (Physics I) + PHYS 0151 or 0171 (Physics II). Option 2: PHYS 0140 + PHYS 0141 + PHYS 3364 Laboratory Electronics.
Select 3 courses from the CBE core: Material and Energy Balances, Thermodynamics of Fluids, Fluid Mechanics, Heat and Mass Transport, Separation Processes, or Chemical Reactor Design.
Select 3 additional courses from CBE core courses OR CBE electives. See department for full elective list.
Select one: CHEM 1011 Introduction to General Chemistry I, CHEM 1012 General Chemistry I, or CHEM 1151 Honors Chemistry I.
Select one: CHEM 1021 Introduction to General Chemistry II, CHEM 1022 General Chemistry II, or CHEM 1161 Honors Chemistry II.
Both CHEM 1101 and CHEM 1102 General Chemistry Laboratory are required (counted as 1 combined CU).
Select 4 CHEM courses at the 2000-level or above. At least one must be an upper-level CHEM lab. CHEM 2999 and CHEM 3999 may NOT be counted. Other departments' courses may not be substituted.
Both CIMS 1001 Introduction to Cinema Studies and CIMS 1002 Introduction to Media Studies are required.
Select at least 1 course from the History & Geography (ACHG) attribute.
Select at least 1 course from the Format & Theory (ACFT) attribute.
Select at least 1 course from the Production & Practice (ACPR) attribute. At least one of the 4 electives must be a seminar at 3000-level or above.

sectionName: Free Elective (any ACHG, ACFT, or ACPR course)
note: Select 1 additional elective from any of the three CIMS attribute lists (ACHG, ACFT, ACPR). At least one of the 4 total electives must be a 3000+ level seminar.

Select 5 courses from ANCH 0000-4999, CLST 0000-4999, GREK 0000-4999, LATN 0000-4999, courses with AANP/ACLB attributes, or any course approved by Undergraduate Chair.
Select one section of Introduction to Cognitive Science (all are the same course with different department codes).

Select 1 course from each of 3 different areas: Psychology (PSYC 0001, PSYC 1310), Computation (CIS 1100, CIS 1200), Language (LING 0001), Philosophy (PHIL 1170, PHIL 2640, PHIL 4660, PHIL 4840), or Neuroscience (NRSC 2249, NRSC 1110, PSYC 1230).
Select 2 courses from the Cognitive Neuroscience (ACGN) concentration. Choose ONE concentration total (ACGN, ACGC, or ACGL). Consult Undergraduate Chair.
Select 2 courses from the Computation and Cognition (ACGC) concentration. Choose ONE concentration total. Consult Undergraduate Chair.
Select 2 courses from the Language and Mind (ACGL) concentration. Choose ONE concentration total. Consult Undergraduate Chair.
Select one: COML 1000 Introduction to Literary Study or COML 1025 Narrative Across Cultures.
COML 1400 Introduction to Literary Theory is required.
Select 1 course with a COML number.
Select 3 courses in any national or comparative literature (any department). The 3 electives may be from any literature/film department — COML, ENGL, FREN, GRMN, ITAL, SPAN, EALC, etc.
NRSC 1110 Introduction to Brain and Behavior is required.
Select one: CIS 1100 Introduction to Computer Programming or CIS 1200 Programming Languages and Techniques I.
Select one section of Theoretical and Computational Neuroscience (same course, multiple codes).
Independent Research in NRSC, COGS, CIS, BE, PHYS, BIOL, or other relevant major. Must be arranged with a faculty supervisor.

Select 4 elective course units. See https://neuroscience.sas.upenn.edu/studying-neuroscience/requirements/computational-neuroscience-minor for approved elective list. No more than 5 of the 8 total courses may double-count toward another major or minor.
CIS 1200 Programming Languages and Techniques I, CIS 1210 Programming Languages and Techniques II, and CIS 1600 Mathematical Foundations of Computer Science are all required.
CIS 1100 Introduction to Computer Programming may be included only if taken before CIS 1200.
Select 1 CIS or NETS engineering course at the 1000 level or above (except CIS 1810), or ESE 3500. Not all CIS/NETS courses are engineering courses — see SEAS Undergraduate Handbook.
Select 1 CIS or NETS engineering course at the 2000 level or above. Not all CIS/NETS courses are engineering courses — see SEAS Undergraduate Handbook.
Select 1 statistics course.
PSYC 0001 Introduction to Experimental Psychology is required.
Select one: PSYC 1440 Social Psychology OR PSYC 2400 Introduction to Positive Psychology.
Select one cognition/decision course.

Select 1 course with the APCP attribute not already used above. May also be any 4000-level PSYC course in Social Psychology or Decision Making. Two of the four PSYC courses must count ONLY toward the Consumer Psychology minor.
MKTG 1010 Introduction to Marketing is required.
MKTG 2110 Consumer Behavior is required.
Select 1 analytics/methods marketing course.
Select course(s) totaling 1 CU with the APCN attribute — either one 1.0 CU course or two 0.5 CU courses. Special topics MKTG 3000-series may also count with approval.
Select 4 workshop courses with the AECW (Creative Writing Workshop) attribute. 1 course unit may be taken in the literature of a language other than English if approved by the advisor.
Select 2 courses in the study of literature (any national or comparative literature). 1 of the 2 may be in the literature of a language other than English if approved by the advisor.
Select 1 introductory data science and programming course. R-track options: CRIM 4002, LING 0700, PSCI 1800. Python-track options: CIS 1050, COMM 3180, COMM 2550, ENGL 1670, PHYS 1100, PHYS 2260.
Select 1 math and statistics course.

Select 1 applied data science course. R-track options: BIOL 4511, CRIM 4012, ECON 4330, GAFL 5310, PSCI 3800, STAT 4230, STAT 4420, STAT 4710, STAT 4700, STAT 4730, PSCI 4811, STAT 4230. Python-track options: CIS 4190, CIS 5450, ESE 2000, ESE 3600, PHYS 2200, PHYS 3359.
Select 3 elective courses with a strong link to data science. Course exceptions may be approved by contacting joellegr@sas.upenn.edu.
CIS 1200 Programming Languages and Techniques I is required.
Select one: CIS 4190/5190 Applied Machine Learning, STAT 4710 Modern Data Mining, or CIS 5200 Machine Learning. Note: CIS 4190/5190 and CIS 5200 cannot both be taken for credit toward the minor.
Select one: NETS 2120 Scalable and Cloud Computing or CIS 5450 Big Data Analytics.
Select one: ESE 3010 Engineering Probability, ESE 4020 Statistics for Data Science, STAT 4300 Probability, or STAT 4310 Statistical Inference.

Select 1 elective from Data Collection, Representation, Management, and Retrieval category.
Select 1 elective from Data Analysis category. STAT 4220 is a 0.5 CU course.
Select 1 elective from Modeling category. Two electives total are required, chosen from two different categories above.

Select 2 electives from Design Studio (DSGN 0000-2999), Fine Arts Studio (FNAR 0000-2999), or courses with the ADEL attribute.

ENGL 1670 is the default Tier 1 second course. CIS 1050 or CIS 1100 may substitute.

Select 1-3 Tier 3 courses with the ADHE (Digital Humanities Research) attribute or from approved DH electives. Tier 3 may also be fulfilled with a significant digital project in a non-DH course with director approval, or a project-centered independent study.
CIS 1400 Introduction to Cognitive Science is required.
CIS 1200, CIS 1210, and CIS 1600 are all required. CIS 1100 may be taken before CIS 1200 (counts as optional prior prep; if not taken, minor is 7 CU total instead of 8).
Select one: CIS 4600 or CIS 5600 Interactive Computer Graphics.
Select one: CIS 3500 Software Design/Engineering, CIS 4610/5610 Advanced Rendering, or CIS 4620/5620 Computer Animation. Program approval required for exceptions.

note: Select 1 upper-level Earth and Environmental Science course approved by advisor. Must not overlap with courses used in other sections.

note: Select at least 1 course from Advanced Language or History/Culture Survey or Literature/Film Survey categories.

NURS 5430 Global Health Seminar is required. Open to Penn Nursing students only (undergraduate and graduate). Contact globalhealth@nursing.upenn.edu for more information.
NURS 5000 Introduction to Principles and Methods of Epidemiology is required.

note: Select at least 1 course from Literature or Written Culture field.

note: Select at least 1 course from Religion, Philosophy, or Science field.

At least 1 course must focus primarily on an area outside of Europe (attribute AGMO in the program's notation). Students must take 6 courses total satisfying at least 3 of 4 fields AND both geographic requirements.

note: Select one: SPAN 1800 Contexts of Hispanic Culture and Civilization OR SPAN 1900 Ways of Reading. Must be taken in the Department at Penn.

note: Select 3 courses at the 3000 level. At least 2 must be taken in the Department at Penn. Up to 2 courses may be from an approved study abroad program. All courses must be taught in Spanish.

note: Select 4 courses at the 2000-level or above with the AHAE (ARTH Elective Major/Minor) attribute. One course may be in a related field outside Art History, subject to Undergraduate Chair approval.

At least 4 of 6 courses must be taken in the Penn History Dept. Courses may not be taken P/F.

Select 1 HIST course without the AH18 attribute (i.e., post-1800). All HIST 2000-3799 range courses without AH18 qualify.

minorName: History, Health and the Humanities, Minor
note: NURS 3050 Narrative Matters in Health and Illness Experiences is required. Open to Penn Nursing undergraduate students.

minorName: History, Health and the Humanities, Minor

note: Select 5 elective CU from approved humanities, history, and health courses. At least 1 elective must be in the School of Nursing. Full approved list at https://www.nursing.upenn.edu/academics/bachelor-of-science-in-nursing-bsn/minors/. Contact akachmar@nursing.upenn.edu.

note: Select 2 free electives with AIRE, AIRH, AIRN, AIRP, or AIRS attribute. Only 1 non-College course may count toward the entire minor. We do not accept petitions for the minor.

ITAL 1000 Advanced Italian I is required.

note: Select 2 courses from ITAL 1890 or seminars taught in Italian at 3000-level or higher.

Full course list at http://ccat.sas.upenn.edu/jwst/undergraduate.

note: Select 1 course with the AARC (ARCH Undergraduate Culture, Society, and the City) attribute.

minorName: Latin American and Latinx Studies, Minor
note: Language competence equal to two semesters of college study in Spanish or Portuguese is required (equivalent to completing SPAN 0200 or PRTG 0200 level). This requirement is typically satisfied by prior language study.

minorName: Latin American and Latinx Studies, Minor
note: Select 6 courses with Latin American content (ALMR or other LALS attributes: ALCR, ALDI, ALHI, ALRP, ALSS). All LALS cross-listed courses qualify. Only 2 courses counted toward a major may be double-counted. Study abroad courses may count with advisor approval.

STSC 0100 Emergence of Modern Science and STSC 0600 Technology & Society are required.

minorName: Russian Language, Literature and Culture, Minor
note: Language prerequisite: 4 semesters or equivalent in Russian: RUSS 0100, RUSS 0200, RUSS 0300, RUSS 0400 or RUSS 0401. Listed as prerequisite, not part of the 6 course units.

minorName: Russian Language, Literature and Culture, Minor
note: Catalog lists REES 0010 Central and Eastern Europe: Cultures, Histories, Societies OR any REES course between 0000-0099.

minorName: Russian Language, Literature and Culture, Minor
note: Catalog lists RUSS 1100 and RUSS 1200, or any RUSS course between 1000-4999, or any REES course between 2100-2699 or 3100-3699.

minorName: Russian and East European Studies, Minor
note: Catalog lists REES 0010 Central and Eastern Europe: Cultures, Histories, Societies OR any REES course between 0000-0099.
PSYC 0001 Introduction to Experimental Psychology is required.

sectionName: Distribution I: Biological Basis of Behavior
note: Select one Biological Basis of Behavior course.

sectionName: Distribution II: Cognitive Basis of Behavior
note: Select one Cognitive Basis of Behavior course.

sectionName: Distribution III: Individual & Group Bases of Behavior
note: Select one Individual & Group Bases of Behavior course.
Select one approved statistics course. Required: minimum GPA of 2.0 in the minor upon graduation.
Select one introductory Political Science core course. Catalog typo says 'Polotical Science Core Requirement.'
Option I lists PHYS 0150, PHYS 0151, PHYS 1230, PHYS 1250, PHYS 2280, plus one PHYS course at the 3000 level or above. Total 6 courses; no more than two courses may be at the 1000 introductory level.
Required language sequence: Beginning Yiddish I, Beginning Yiddish II, Intermediate Yiddish I, and Intermediate Yiddish II.
Choose two from the listed electives. Catalog also states that transfer or study abroad credit for courses taught in Yiddish may count.
URBS 3000 Urban Internship Seminar is required, or an approved alternative work option, for 2 course units.

minorName: Urban Real Estate and Development, Minor
note: Select two URBS Core courses. Take either URBS 2040 Urban Law or REAL 2040 Real Estate Law, not both.

minorName: Urban Real Estate and Development, Minor
note: Select two REAL Core courses. Take either URBS 2040 Urban Law or REAL 2040 Real Estate Law, not both.

All students take URBS 2020 Urban Education, select 1 Child or Adolescent Development course, and complete a URED Capstone. The catalog page does not enumerate the Child/Adolescent Development options; the capstone is taken as a Senior Thesis course or Independent Study.

sectionName: Urban Education Policy, Research, and Practice Track
note: Track requires 1 Urban Context course, 1 Teaching and Learning course, 1 ABCS course related to Urban Education, and 1 of: Policy Elective course, EDUC 5335, or another elective course. Catalog does not enumerate the Urban Context, Teaching and Learning, ABCS, Policy Elective, or other elective lists.

Elementary Education Track lists EDUC 4014 Children's Literature, EDUC 5020 Literacy in Elementary/Middle Schools, and a methods choice between EDUC 5021 Science Methods: Project-Based Learning Approaches or EDUC 5023 Social Studies in the Elementary and Middle Schools. The catalog table shows this track as part of the four track-specific course units.

Secondary Education Track requires 1 Pedagogy & Curriculum (ABCS) course, 1 Content Area (ABCS) course, one course from EDUC 6027 or EDUC 6329, and EDUC 6057. Catalog does not enumerate the Pedagogy & Curriculum or Content Area ABCS options.
Required courses: ESE 2100 Introduction to Dynamic Systems; ESE 2240 Signal and Information Processing; ESE 3030 Stochastic Systems Analysis and Simulation; ESE 3040 Introduction to Optimization.
ENVS 1000 Introduction to Environmental Science is required.
Take one of EAS 4010 Energy and Its Impacts, EAS 4020 Renewable Energy and Its Impacts, or EAS 4030 Energy Systems and Policy.
Take any 2 of LGST 2150 Environmental Management: Law & Policy, BEPP 2610 Risk Analysis and Environmental Management, or BEPP 2630 Environmental & Energy Economics and Policy.

note: Select 1 American Politics course.

note: Select 1-2 course units of Statistics. The STAT 1010 & STAT 1020 sequence and STAT 1110 & STAT 1120 sequence are listed as paired options.

minorName: Survey Research and Data Analytics, Minor

minorName: Survey Research and Data Analytics, Minor
note: Select 3 course units of electives with Attribute APSU (PSCI Minor Survey Research and Analytics).
Select one calculus option: MATH 1410, MATH 1080, or MATH 1610.
Select one introductory statistics/statistical inference option.
Select one probability option.
Additionally select 4 course units from the listed STAT electives.
Select 2 foundational courses from the listed SAST options.

Catalog says select 4 course units relevant to South Asia in these ranges: ARAB 0300-0600, BENG 0300-1500, GURJ 0300-1502, HIND 0300-1500, KAND 0300-1500, MLYM 0300-1500, MRTI 0300-1500, PASH 0300-1500, PERS 0300-0400/0500-0600/2000-2100, PUNJ 0300-1500, SKRT 0300-0400, TAML 0300-0400, TELU 0300-0400, URDU 0300-1500. Options here include confirmed catalog courses fetched in this batch from accessible course pages for ARAB, BENG, HIND, PERS, SKRT, and URDU; several small language course pages were unavailable through the catalog fetch and should be expanded when accessible.
